question: Will any of these AI companies (Lovable, DeepSeek, or Perplexity) have a funding round in July 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as **Yes** if any of these companies has a funding round listed by TracXn with a date in July 2026: [Lovable](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/lovable/__fO6WUvzcx5q3814ietMd3kzGlRirsatzsTnpxmzLuMI/funding-and-investors),  [DeepSeek](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/deepseek/__1GrZ3pgoi2O-9tMSfF9ka6Sjybc1lgSSZ_qQqo5k2vE/funding-and-investors), or [Perplexity](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/perplexity/__V2BE-5ihMWJ1hNb2_u1W7Gry25JzPFCBg-iNWi94XI8/funding-and-investors).
reference_class: Active late-stage AI unicorns' funding round closing within a specific ~1-week window given an already-public 'in talks' process
reasoning:
Base rate: for a specific ~6-day window, the chance any one of three highly active AI companies logs a new TracXn-dated round is low unless a deal is already imminent. DeepSeek's first-ever $7.4B round closed mid-June 2026 (Tencent/CATL-led) — done before the window opens. Perplexity's most recent raise (~$200M for Comet) was reported June 5-8, 2026, also pre-window, and July coverage frames Perplexity's month as product-focused, not fundraising. Lovable is the live wildcard: in talks since June 5, 2026 for a $12B valuation round (up from $6.6B), but as of the latest available reporting the round remained unclosed, with the company declining to confirm terms. Given active negotiations, some chance it closes in the first days of July, but no evidence yet that it has. Aggregating: low but non-trivial probability, driven almost entirely by uncertainty over whether the Lovable round (or an unreported event) closes by July 6, 21:00 UTC.
[pooled 3 independent runs [0.09, 0.08, 0.07] -> 0.080; the narrative above is the research run's own view (0.09)]
what_would_change_my_mind: A press release or Tracxn listing showing Lovable's $12B round has closed with a July 2026 date
Any news of a surprise new Perplexity or DeepSeek round announced this week
Confirmation that DeepSeek's June round has a secondary July closing/tranche date on Tracxn

missing_evidence: No reporting found between June 8 and July 6, 2026 on Lovable round status, and Tracxn's own funding pages were inaccessible (403) to verify exact listed dates or date-attribution convention (announcement vs. close).
Could not access Tracxn's pages directly (403 blocked) to check exact date attribution conventions or any already-listed July entries

question: Will any of these AI companies (OpenAI, Anthropic, or Mistral AI) have a funding round in July 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as **Yes** if any of these companies has a funding round listed by TracXn with a date in July 2026: [Mistral AI](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/mistral-ai/__SLZq7rzxLYqqA97jtPwO09jLDeb76RVJVb306OhciWU/funding-and-investors#funding-rounds),  [Anthropic](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/anthropic/__SzoxXDMin-NK5tKB7ks8yHr6S9Mz68pjVCzFEcGFZ08/funding-and-investors#funding-rounds), or [OpenAI](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/openai/__kElhSG7uVGeFk1i71Co9-nwFtmtyMVT7f-YHMn4TFBg/funding-and-investors).
reference_class: Frontier AI lab (OpenAI/Anthropic/Mistral) mega-funding-round cadence in the 2026 AI funding boom
reasoning:
Anthropic just closed Series H (Tracxn-dated Apr 21, 2026) and is now on an IPO track (confidential S-1 filed June 1, 2026), explicitly framed as its last private round — new dated entry in July is unlikely (~10%). OpenAI's Series F round has shown a pattern of new dated tranches on Tracxn as fresh investors join (Feb 27, Mar 20, Apr 22, 2026) but nothing since — a ~10-week gap as of now; SoftBank's already-committed $10B tranche executed July 1 is unlikely to appear as a new distinct entry since anchor investors were dated at initial close (~20%). Mistral's rumored €3B/€20B round was only in 'early discussions' as of June 12, 2026 with no confirmed terms or close date — plausible to close in July given competitive urgency but more likely slips later (~22%). Combining three roughly independent, individually sub-50% chances yields an aggregate around 40%.
[pooled 3 independent runs [0.4, 0.4, 0.35] -> 0.383; the narrative above is the research run's own view (0.40)]
what_would_change_my_mind: Confirmation Mistral's €3B round has signed definitive terms/closed
News of a new OpenAI investor tranche or Series G announcement
Any report Anthropic is doing a private round despite IPO filing
Direct Tracxn page update showing a July 2026 dated entry for any of the three

missing_evidence: Could not directly load Tracxn's live funding-and-investors pages (blocked, 403) to check for any new dated entries since the Apr 2026 snapshot; Tracxn's exact methodology for whether follow-on tranches to existing investors generate new discrete round entries (vs. only updating an existing round's

No direct evidence on Tracxn's internal methodology for whether follow-on SoftBank tranches of an already-recorded OpenAI commitment get separate dated entries; no confirmed timeline for Mistral round closing.

question: What will be the result of the 2026 Florida Republican gubernatorial primary?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as the result of the [2026 Republican primary](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Florida_gubernatorial_election#Republican_primary) in the Florida gubernatorial election, according to official results or [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions).
options: Donalds wins, with ≥75% of the vote
Donalds wins, with >65% and <75% of the vote
Donalds wins, with >55% and ≤65% of the vote
Donalds wins, with ≤55% of the vote
reasoning: Byron Donalds has near-total institutional consolidation (Trump, Rick Scott, House leadership) against a fractured, weak field (Collins, Fishback, Renner combined ~15% decided support). Polling rose from 46% (Mar) to 54% (Jun) with 31-39% undecided; late-breaking undecideds in blowout primaries typically favor the recognized frontrunner disproportionately, pointing to a final vote share likely in the mid-60s to low-70s. Polymarket prices Donalds winning outright at ~95-96%, consistent with a very low probability of an outright loss. Five internal draws across trend-extrapolation, historical-comparison, weak-opposition, downside-risk, and market-anchored framings centered on 63-72% vote share, with the 65-75% bucket modal.

question: How many EU member states will the AI Act National Implementation tracker classify as having clear market surveillance and notifying authorities on August 31, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as the number of National Competent Authorities classified as "Clear" at the EU's  [Overview of all AI Act National Implementation Plans](https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/national-implementation-plans/) tracker, when checked by Metaculus on or after August 31, 2026.
reasoning: Tracker (artificialintelligenceact.eu) shows 9/27 states 'Clear' as of last update 17 June 2026, essentially unchanged from ~March 2026 (8-9), indicating a multi-month plateau. Statutory deadline was Aug 2025; compliance has been chronically slow (EU cross-border regulatory harmonization historically drags for years, cf. GDPR SAs). France dropped its designation provisions from pending legislation. August is EU summer-recess season, reducing odds of a burst of national legislative action before Aug 31. Modest upward drift (to 10-11) is plausible as 1-2 'partial clarity' states finalize, but a jump to 12+ within under 8 weeks would require an unprecedented acceleration versus the observed flat trend.

question: What will be the World Bank monthly price of liquid sulphur (Tampa) for Q3 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as the price of liquid sulphur (Tampa) for Q3 2026, according to [Argus Media](https://www.argusmedia.com/en/commodities/sulphur-and-sulphuric-acid).
reasoning: Tampa quarterly contract rose Q4'25 $310 -> Q1'26 ~$500 -> Q2'26 $655/lt CFR (~30% q/q gains) as global sulfur benchmarks spiked on Strait of Hormuz disruption. Middle East OSPs for July loading (Kuwait $950/t, Abu Dhabi $1,000/t fob) jumped another $140-145 from June, and marginal spot sulfur was near $1,200/t as of May. Since Tampa contracts settle early in the quarter, this July spike likely feeds directly into the Q3 settlement, implying a further large rise over $655. Countervailing: floating storage in the Gulf has drained from ~1mt to 300-400kt as vessels resume Hormuz transits, and a holding ceasefire could compress the risk premium within Q3, with World Bank commentary suggesting stabilization is expected only after Q3. Median draw ~$850-870 balances continued momentum against a real chance of partial reversal or a further overshoot toward/above the $1,100 bound.

question: Will any U.S. Supreme Court justice announce their retirement before September 1, 2026?
resolution_criterion:
This question resolves as **Yes** if any of these Justices before September 1, 2026 announces or confirms their intention to retire, resign or permanently leave active service on the United States Supreme Court:

* Chief Justice John Roberts
* Associate Justice Clarence Thomas
* Associate Justice Samuel Alito
* Associate Justice Sonia Sotomayor
* Associate Justice Elena Kagan
* Associate Justice Neil Gorsuch
* Associate Justice Brett Kavanaugh
* Associate Justice Amy Coney Barrett
* Associate Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson
reference_class: SCOTUS justice retirement/resignation announcements per 2-month window, unconditional historical rate across sitting justices
reasoning: Base rate for any SCOTUS retirement announcement in a random ~8-week window is low (~4-5%, roughly 1 announcement per 3-4 years across 9 justices). This window is elevated above base rate: Alito (76) and Thomas (78) are near/past the modern average retirement age, intense speculation peaked with a (retracted) false NPR report on 6/30/26, and strategic logic favors retiring before the Nov 2026 midterms in case the GOP loses the Senate. Against that: sources close to both justices explicitly deny plans to retire 'this year' (CBS, ABC, July 2026), and Polymarket's Alito-specific market prices only ~46% cumulative probability of an announcement by Dec 31 2026 — a 6-month window nearly 3x longer than the one at issue here, implying a materially smaller share falls before Sept 1. No other justice shows any credible near-term retirement signal (Barrett faces partisan pressure but no personal intent signal; liberal justices have no incentive to retire under a GOP-controlled White House/Senate).
what_would_change_my_mind: Any on-the-record statement from a justice's office confirming retirement intent
A confirmed leak of a retirement letter (as happened with O'Connor/Breyer)
Senate Judiciary Committee scheduling unexplained confirmation-track activity
A sharp shift in the Polymarket Alito/Thomas date-bracket odds toward near-term brackets

question: Will France have more than one confirmed case of Ebola on September 1, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as **Yes** if before September 1, 2026, France has more than 1 confirmed case of Ebola in the 2026 outbreak, according to the [European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control](https://www.ecdc.europa.eu/en/ebola-outbreak-democratic-republic-congo-and-uganda) (ECDC).
reference_class: Countries outside Africa receiving imported Ebola cases during large West/Central African Ebola outbreaks, and whether they then accumulate a 2nd+ case via further import or local transmission
reasoning:
France's only confirmed case (doctor, confirmed 2026-06-24) recovered and was discharged by 2026-07-04 with no secondary transmission found among ~5 monitored contacts — the transmission-from-case-1 pathway is now nearly closed. The remaining path to 'Yes' is a fresh independent import: the DRC/Uganda outbreak is large (1,561+20 cases) and still accelerating (+564 confirmed in DRC in ~2 weeks), and France (via NGOs like Alima) has meaningful traveler/aid-worker flow to DRC, similar to what produced case 1. But base rate is low: in the 2014-16 West Africa epidemic (much larger, longer-running), France recorded zero cases despite 28,000+ regional cases; only countries with direct medevac or lapses in case detection (US, Spain) saw secondary infections, and only after health-system contact failures rather than immediate isolation like France's case 1. With ~8 weeks left and continued ECDC 'very low' community risk rating, I put full-outbreak-in-France reaching >1 confirmed case at ~8%.
[pooled 3 independent runs [0.08, 0.12, 0.08] -> 0.092; the narrative above is the research run's own view (0.08)]
what_would_change_my_mind: ECDC or French health ministry reporting a new symptomatic returnee testing positive
Any of the ~5 monitored contacts from case 1 developing confirmed Ebola
A sharp escalation in DRC outbreak size/velocity that increases aid-worker deployment and return-travel volume from France
News of poor infection-control practices at the hospital that treated case 1

missing_evidence: No data found on the current number of French nationals/aid workers actively deployed in DRC/Uganda outbreak zones, which would sharpen the imported-case rate estimate.
No specific data found on current volume of French nationals/aid workers deployed to or returning from DRC/Uganda outbreak zones, which would directly inform import risk over the remaining window.

question: Will the Lebanese Armed Forces deploy to the initial two pilot zones in southern Lebanon before September 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question will resolve as **Yes** if the Lebanese Armed Forces have physically deployed military units to the two initial pilot zones specified in the June 26, 2026 Washington trilateral framework agreement for a minimum of seven days before 12:01am ET on September 1, 2026, according to official statements from the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL), the Lebanese Armed Forces or credible reporting from Reuters or the Associated Press.
reference_class: Phased troop withdrawal/deployment milestones in the 2024-2026 Israel-Lebanon ceasefire process
reasoning:
Status quo as of July 8: framework signed June 26; no physical LAF deployment yet; initial informal target (~June 29) already missed. Reference class: phased withdrawal/deployment steps in the 2024-2026 Lebanon ceasefire process have historically slipped by weeks-to-months (IDF withdrawal deadlines extended repeatedly). Base rate for a fragile first-phase pilot deployment amid explicit non-state-actor rejection landing within ~7 weeks: roughly 35-40%. Update: strong countervailing factors exist (US/CENTCOM pressure, small symbolic scope of only two towns, both governments' incentive to show the framework holds after a costly war), pulling above a pure historical-slippage base rate. But offsetting negative factors dominate: Hezbollah has explicitly vowed to block implementation, Israel conditions withdrawal on full clearance with no fixed date, sequencing is deadlocked, and the environment is destabilized by the recent Khamenei assassination/war and ongoing funeral proceedings. Net: modestly below even odds that a full 7-day deployment completes before Aug 25.
[pooled 3 independent runs [0.36, 0.12, 0.15] -> 0.192; the narrative above is the research run's own view (0.36)]
what_would_change_my_mind: Official LAF or IDF announcement of a firm deployment date within the pilot zones
UNIFIL or CENTCOM confirmation that Israeli withdrawal from Zawtar/Froun has begun
Signs Hezbollah has quietly vacated the two pilot zones without incident
Renewed large-scale fighting or a fresh Israeli-Hezbollah escalation that would freeze all diplomatic implementation
A firm sequencing/clearance agreement reached via the Military Coordination Group for Lebanon (MCG4L)

missing_evidence: No confirmed deployment date from LAF/UNIFIL/Reuters/AP found; no liquid prediction market pricing this question was located.
No official confirmed deployment date, no UNIFIL statement on the pilot zones specifically, and no market/crowd pricing for this question were found.

question: What will be the highest price of copper futures in August 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question will resolve as the highest price of copper (HG\=F) in USD per lb displayed by [Yahoo Finance](https://uk.finance.yahoo.com/quote/HG%3DF/history/) for any day in August 2026.
reasoning: Base rate: monthly high tends to sit modestly above the prior month's high in an uptrend; 2026 has been a rising-price year (Jan high $5.655 -> June high $6.653), driven by US tariff uncertainty on copper imports and steady industrial/EV/grid demand. Absent a specific August catalyst, the distribution centers slightly above the June high with wide uncertainty from tariff-news volatility (copper has shown large single-day swings around tariff announcements in 2026). Downside risk: demand slowdown or tariff resolution reducing front-running premium. Upside risk: further tariff escalation or supply disruption pushing new highs above $6.8, hence a fat right tail even though point estimates must stay inside the stated (5.6, 6.8) bound.

question: How many ETFs will Vanguard have on September 1, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as the number of results at this page, when accessed by Metaculus on or after September 1, 2026: [Vanguard ETFs](https://investor.vanguard.com/investment-products/list/etfs?filters=open)
reference_class: Vanguard US ETF launch cadence, 2026 YTD
reasoning: Question's own fine print confirms 116 results as of June 23, 2026, already incorporating the March bond-suite (10 ETFs), April VDV/VDG, and June VCHY launches. Remaining uncertainty is entirely about net launches/closures in the ~10-week window to Sept 1. Vanguard's 2026 cadence has been unusually active (13 new US ETFs Jan-June, ~0.5-2/month depending on whether the one-time bond-suite batch is included), but targeted searches found no confirmed specific launch scheduled for July or August 2026. Vanguard has essentially never closed a US ETF (one liquidation ever, 2022), making 'less than 116' very unlikely. Distributed roughly evenly across no-change/+1/+2+ given genuine uncertainty about whether the active pace continues into this specific window.
what_would_change_my_mind: A Vanguard press release or SEC 8-A filing announcing a new US ETF launch dated between July and August 2026
News of a Vanguard US ETF liquidation/closure announcement
Direct access to the resolution page showing a live count closer to Sept 1

question: Will the Chinese Foreign Minister visit South Korea before September 1, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as **Yes** if, after June 30, 2026 and before September 1, 2026, the Chinese Foreign Minister personally visits South Korea.
reference_class: Previously-announced-but-repeatedly-postponed bilateral FM visits with an active unresolved diplomatic dispute, resolving within ~2 months
reasoning:
Wang Yi's Seoul visit has been in limbo since ~April 2026 when China put it 'on hold' over Korea's removal of the 'China (Taiwan)' label from its e-arrival card — this follows an earlier Feb 2026 postponement (Spring Festival/Two Sessions conflicts) and no visit has occurred since Nov 2023. As of the latest reporting found (through late June/early July 2026), no dates are finalized and the dispute appears unresolved. Wang Yi's confirmed travel since April (DPRK, Canada/UN, India-BRICS, four Nordic countries in early July) contains no Korea stop, and China is hosting APEC 2026 (Shenzhen, November) — the natural focal point for China-Korea high-level exchange this year is pointing toward autumn, not summer. Base rate: repeated multi-month slippages on this specific visit plus a live unresolved diplomatic irritant argue for a low probability within the ~7.5-week window remaining.
[pooled 4 independent runs [0.08, 0.06, 0.08, 0.06] -> 0.069; the narrative above is the research run's own view (0.08)]
what_would_change_my_mind: Official announcement from Chinese or Korean MFA confirming specific dates for a Wang Yi Seoul visit
Resolution of the Taiwan e-arrival-card labeling dispute (Korea reverting label or Taiwan dropping its retaliatory 'South Korea' relabeling)
News of a Cho Hyun trip to Beijing explicitly framed as paving the way for a near-term Wang Yi reciprocal visit
Any Chinese MFA diplomatic schedule listing Wang Yi's departure for Seoul before Sept 1

missing_evidence: No direct confirmation on whether informal planning/back-channel talks for a rescheduled visit are underway in July-August 2026
No direct confirmation on whether the Taiwan protocol dispute has any resolution track underway as of early July 2026

No direct confirmation either way on whether the Taiwan-labeling dispute has been quietly resolved in the last 1-2 weeks (searches found nothing past early July 2026 specific to this dispute's status)

question: Will Apple or Meta sign the European Commission's AI Pact before September 1, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as **Yes** if, before September 1, 2026, Apple or Meta appear at the AI Pact [Signatories of the pledges](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/ai-pact#ecl-inpage-Signatories-of-the-AI-Pact) list maintained by the European Commission.
reference_class: Major US tech firms (Apple, Meta) that were day-one holdouts from a voluntary EU pledge scheme and later joined within ~2 years
reasoning: Apple and Meta have refused to sign the EU AI Pact since its Sept 2024 launch — nearly 22 months of consistent holdout with no signs of change. Base rate: of the ~2 major holdouts among ~15-20 large tech/AI companies, 0 have joined in ~22 months, an implied monthly join-probability near 0. Rather than warming, relations have sharply deteriorated in 2026: both companies were fined under the DMA (Apple €500M, Meta €200M) in April 2026, Apple lost its DMA legal challenge in EU General Court on 2026-07-08, Apple has publicly demanded the DMA be repealed, and Meta separately refused to sign the related (also voluntary) EU AI Code of Practice in July 2025 citing 'legal uncertainties' and regulatory overreach. Meta's own language ('we don't rule out joining... at a later stage') has not translated into any action in 22 months. No credible reporting suggests either company is near signing before Sept 1, 2026 (~53 days from now). Some slight residual probability for an unexpected policy pivot amid the broader EU-US trade/tariff tension, but the trend clearly points away from signing.
what_would_change_my_mind: Either company issuing a public statement of intent to sign the AI Pact
A de-escalation in the EU-Apple/Meta DMA/DSA legal disputes that removes the adversarial backdrop
The European Commission press office confirming a new major-tech addition to the signatory list
Political trade deal between the US and EU that includes tech regulatory concessions, prompting a goodwill gesture from Apple/Meta

question: Will South Korea sign arms export deals worth >$5bn with any country between July 1 and August 31, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as **Yes** if, after June 30, 2026 and before September 1, 2026, the total worth of arms export deals between the Republic of Korea (ROK) and any single country exceeds $5 billion USD, according to [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions). In order to qualify, a deal has to be a binding agreement for a ROK party to sell military equipment or weapons to a counterparty of another country and has to be announced or disclosed by a ROK party.
reference_class: 2-month windows since mid-2022 where cumulative ROK arms deals with one country exceeded $5bn
reasoning:
Base rate for SK crossing $5bn cumulative with one country in a 2-month window is ~8-12% historically (Poland 2022 framework, Aug 2025 $6.7bn K2 deal are the main precedents). This window opened right after SK lost its two largest pending mega-deals to competitors (Canada submarine to Germany, July 6 2026; Poland Orka submarine to Sweden, June 29 2026) — a bearish signal. Poland-Korea dealmaking pace remains high and a 3rd K2PL tank contract (~210 tanks, plausibly $7-8bn) is under active discussion, but no confirmed signing date exists for July-Aug specifically, and MSPO 2026 (where big announcements often cluster) falls just after the window (Sept 8-11). No other single deal (UAE KF-21 $15bn, Egypt FA-50, Iraq air defense) looks close enough to signature within 2 months.
[pooled 3 independent runs [0.15, 0.18, 0.3] -> 0.203; the narrative above is the research run's own view (0.15)]
what_would_change_my_mind: Polish MoD or Hyundai Rotem announcing a signing date for the 3rd K2PL executive contract
News of UAE KF-21 co-development deal reaching binding signature
Egypt FA-50 100-unit deal moving from talks to signed contract
Any surprise large deal with a new customer (Vietnam, Saudi Arabia, Thailand)

missing_evidence: No confirmed signing date for the 3rd K2 executive contract; unclear whether the informal briefing stage typically precedes signing by weeks or months in past rounds
No confirmed signing date or MOU-to-contract timeline for the K2 3rd executive contract

question: Will any of these gubernatorial or gubernatorial primary debates be held before September 1, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as **Yes** if before September 1, 2026, there is one formal, live debate held between any of these candidates: James Fishback and Byron Donalds, Byron Donalds and David Jolly, Josh Shapiro and Stacy Garrity, Tina Kotek and Christine Drazan, Katie Hobbs and the GOP nominee for Arizona governor, or the GOP nominee and the Democratic nominee for Wisconsin governor.
reference_class: Scheduled/organized gubernatorial general and primary debates relative to primary and general election dates in competitive 2026 cycle races
reasoning:
[pooled 3 independent runs [0.08, 0.06, 0.13] -> 0.086; the narrative below is the research run's own view (0.08)]
All six candidate pairs named in the resolution criteria currently show either no scheduled debate or a scheduled/proposed debate dated in September or October (after the window). Donalds (FL) refuses to debate primary rivals; FL GOP has no primary debate planned; the one organized FL general debate is Oct 15. PA's Shapiro/Garrity have no date set beyond stating willingness. Oregon's Kotek explicitly proposed debates 'starting in September' and just declined a July 24 debate. Arizona's Hobbs won't discuss debating until after the July 21 primary, and no GOP nominee exists yet. Wisconsin's one confirmed general debate is Oct 9, with primaries not until Aug 11. Base rate for gubernatorial debates happening this early (before Labor Day, before most primaries have even produced nominees) is low, reinforced by this cycle's specific pattern of candidates deferring or refusing debate commitments.
what_would_change_my_mind: An unscheduled/ad-hoc forum or debate announced suddenly involving any of the 6 pairs before Sept 1
Donalds reversing his no-debate stance for the FL primary
AZ or WI parties fast-tracking a debate immediately after their July 21/Aug 11 primaries
Kotek-Drazan moving their 'starting in September' debate earlier than planned

missing_evidence: No exact date yet for any Oregon Kotek-Drazan debate; no post-primary AZ/WI/FL debate scheduling news since primaries haven't occurred yet (AZ Jul21, WI Aug11, FL Aug18)
no direct confirmation of whether any AZ general-election or WI post-primary debate has been informally planned for late August; could not find a dedicated PA debate negotiation status past June 2026

question: Will Anthropic or Mistral AI sign the European Commission's AI Pact before September 1, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as **Yes** if, before September 1, 2026, Anthropic or Mistral AI appear at the AI Pact [Signatories of the pledges](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/ai-pact#ecl-inpage-Signatories-of-the-AI-Pact) list maintained by the European Commission.
reference_class: Companies that signed EU AI Pact voluntary pledges given prior non-signature and no announced intent, over a ~2-month window
reasoning: The question resolves off the EU AI Pact 'Signatories of the pledges' list (230+ signatories, mostly SMEs/enterprises), NOT the separate GPAI Code of Practice (~24-26 signatories) that both Anthropic and Mistral already signed in mid-2025. Direct fetch of the live AI Pact page today shows neither company listed. Mistral has a multi-year documented history of declining the AI Pact specifically (Sept 2024, reaffirmed since) as one of the AI Act's harshest critics, while continuing to lobby for AI Act delay/simplification through 2026. Anthropic has never been reported as signing or planning to sign the AI Pact (distinct from its Code of Practice signature and its separate Mythos/export-control negotiations with the EU). Base rate: with ~53 days left in the window and no reported intent from either company, and both having already made their substantive EU compliance gesture via the Code of Practice, there's little incentive to also sign the largely symbolic Pact. Small residual probability reflects: (1) rolling/continuous nature of the Pact list meaning any company can join anytime with no advance notice, (2) Anthropic's recent pattern of EU goodwill gestures (Mythos access, EU negotiations) that could extend to a low-cost symbolic pledge, (3) possible reporting lag in my search results.
what_would_change_my_mind: An EU Commission press release or update explicitly adding Anthropic or Mistral to the AI Pact signatories page
A public statement from either company announcing intent to join the AI Pact (distinct from Code of Practice)
Renewed EU pressure campaign (e.g., tied to the Anthropic hosting/sovereignty talks) that includes AI Pact signature as a goodwill gesture

question: How many gold medals will Australia win at the 2026 Commonwealth Games?
resolution_criterion: This question will resolve as the total number of gold medals that Team Australia are awarded as of the closing ceremony (August 2, 2026) of the XXIII Commonwealth Games, according to [the official medal table](https://www.glasgow2026.com/medals).
reference_class: Australia's gold medal count in the specific 10 sports/disciplines contested at Glasgow 2026, as won at the 2022 Birmingham Commonwealth Games (same or similar event slate)
reasoning: Glasgow 2026 cuts Australia's program from ~280 to 215 gold events across just 10 sports, but the retained sports (swimming, athletics, track cycling, gymnastics, weightlifting, boxing, judo, bowls, netball, 3x3 basketball) accounted for ~79% (53/67) of Australia's Birmingham 2022 golds, while cut sports (hockey, squash, diving, rugby sevens, cricket, triathlon, road cycling) were only moderate contributors. Event counts within retained sports are similar or slightly larger than in 2022 (para-sport expansion, where Australia is also strong, partly offsets trimming). Bottom-up per-sport modeling (swimming ~45% share of 56 golds ≈25; track cycling ~35-40% share of 26 ≈9-10; athletics ~18% share of 59 ≈11; smaller contributions from judo/gymnastics/weightlifting/boxing/bowls/3x3/netball) and multiple top-down scaling methods converge on a central estimate of 50-55. Strong announced squads (McKeown, Chalmers in swimming; six reigning track cycling champions; deep athletics medal contenders) support the upper-middle of this range. Main counterargument: this format is unprecedented, so historical per-sport shares carry real transfer uncertainty, especially in judging-dependent sports (boxing, judo, weightlifting) and newer para-integrated events.

question: What state will have the lowest temperature on July 31, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as the state with the weather station recording the lowest minimum temperature in the United States according to [Ogimet](https://www.ogimet.com/ranking.phtml.en) on July 31, 2026. A state other than the ones listed resolves as Other.
reference_class: State with the single coldest 24-hour minimum temperature among US weather stations on July 31, per Ogimet ranking, years 2019-2025
reasoning: Historical July 31 base rate (2019-2025, n=5 via Ogimet): Alaska 2/5, New Hampshire 2/5, Minnesota 1/5, Colorado/Nevada 0/5 (though Colorado won nearby dates in other months). Very recent 2026 daily rankings show Alaska (Jun 20, Jul 8) and Colorado (Jul 1, Jul 5) alternating as coldest, an unusually strong recent Colorado run. CPC Week 3-4 outlook calls for a western ridge (above-normal temps, Alaska and CO/UT border) and a developing monsoon over the Four Corners by mid-late July, which should add cloud/moisture to Colorado and suppress its radiational-cooling edge by month's end, while implying a compensating cooler pattern further east that favors NH/MN historically. No market found on Polymarket/Kalshi for this exact state-ranking contract; only unrelated city-level daily temp markets exist, so no market blend applied.
what_would_change_my_mind: CPC 8-14 day update showing the monsoon/ridge pattern breaking down before July 31, keeping Colorado's high valleys dry and clear
A forecast cold-air intrusion into interior Alaska or the Northeast in the final week of July
Ogimet rankings for July 20-30, 2026 showing a consistent single-state leader emerging

sources: Polymarket/Kalshi search — no matching state-level market found

question: What state will have the hottest temperature on July 31, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as the state with the weather station recording the highest maximum temperature in the United States according to [Ogimet](https://www.ogimet.com/ranking.phtml.en) on July 31, 2026. A state other than the three listed resolves as Other.
reference_class: State recording the single hottest SYNOP-network station in the continental US on late-July calendar dates, 2022-2025
reasoning: Reference class: Ogimet's daily hottest-US-station winner on late-July dates in recent years, dominated by AZ (Phoenix Sky Harbor, which won the exact analog date July 31 2025 at 109.9F) with NV (Las Vegas/Laughlin, record 120F in 2024) as the main rival and TX a secondary contender. Death Valley/CA rarely appears since Ogimet uses SYNOP airport/military stations, not COOP climate stations (consistent with the May 31 2026 example sweeping all-Texas, no CA station). Adjusted down from a pure AZ-dominant base rate because 2026 is transitioning to El Nino (~80% NOAA odds), which tends to weaken the Southwest monsoon ridge over AZ and shift the seasonal 'hot spot' north/toward TX per current long-range outlooks, modestly boosting TX and NV relative to a typical year.
what_would_change_my_mind: A GFS/ECMWF 10-14 day run (available ~July 17-21) showing a heat dome centered directly over the Desert Southwest/Phoenix vs. shifted toward the Great Basin/Mojave or Texas
Confirmation of active monsoon thunderstorm/cloud cover over Phoenix specifically on July 31
A specific Las Vegas or Texas station forecast to exceed 115F on July 31

question: How many jobs will the U.S. economy add in July 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as the increase in number of employees on nonfarm payrolls, seasonally adjusted, reported by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics at its[ Employment Situation Summary](https://www.bls.gov/news.release/empsit.nr0.htm) for July 2026.
reference_class: trailing 12-month average monthly change in U.S. seasonally-adjusted nonfarm payrolls, as of the June 2026 BLS report
reasoning: June 2026 NFP was +57k, which BLS characterized as roughly in line with the trailing-12-month average of +36k — a clear anchor for trend growth. But June included an anomalous -61k leisure/hospitality drop that likely partially mean-reverts, and ISM services employment just moved back into expansion (51.2 from 47.9), a mildly positive signal. Offsetting factors: fading state/local government hiring boost (per Capital Economics, though their forecasts have been running too high all year), immigration-curb effects on labor supply/demand, and tariff-related hiring caution. Initial claims stay low (labor hoarding, 'slow-hire, slow-fire') but continuing claims are drifting up. No mature market gives a firm July-specific consensus this early (survey week hasn't occurred); Polymarket's July contract is still pricing bins near-equal. Landed slightly above the 12-month trend base rate to reflect partial reversion from June's outlier weakness, with wide uncertainty band given this cycle's pattern of large forecast misses (both directions).

question: Will Vladimir Putin visit Iran before September 1, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as **Yes** if, after July 15, 2026 and before September 1, 2026, Russian President Vladimir Putin personally visits Iran, according to [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions).
reference_class: Pre-announced Putin international summit attendance (multilateral summits Putin was reported planning to attend >1 month out, 2022-2026)
reasoning:
[pooled 3 independent runs [0.35, 0.34, 0.35] -> 0.347; the narrative below is the research run's own view (0.35)]
[scenario-coherence: p=0.34 leaves 0.34 against the lean, but the run's own counter-scenarios total 0.61]
Base rate for 'multiple pre-announced summit attendance by Putin' when no major disruption is ~0.65-0.75; here that's cut sharply by an acute, resumed shooting war in the host country (ceasefire declared 'over' July 8, 2026, strikes continuing July 9-10) and the precedent that Iran's own Supreme Leader was killed by airstrike in Feb 2026, raising real doubt the Aug 12 Caspian Summit proceeds in Tehran as planned, or that Putin attends in person rather than virtually/via a lower-level delegate. Countervailing: repeated high-level reaffirmation (Kremlin, Iranian ambassador, FM Lavrov) of intent to hold the summit and for Putin to attend, even stated after the Khamenei assassination; a Putin visit could function as an informal deterrent shield since US/Israel would be very unlikely to risk hitting Russia's president. Net: meaningfully below the 'summit happens as intended' base rate but not collapsed, given 7 weeks remain for either de-escalation or further disruption.
what_would_change_my_mind: Kremlin or Iranian government issuing a formal itinerary/confirmation for Putin's Aug 12 attendance
A durable ceasefire or de-escalation restored well before mid-August
Official postponement or venue change of the Caspian Summit
Reports Putin will attend virtually or send Mishustin/Lavrov instead
Further escalation (e.g., strikes on Tehran itself) making the summit untenable

missing_evidence: No reporting found since the July 8-10 ceasefire collapse on whether Iran/Russia still intend to hold the Caspian Summit as scheduled on Aug 12, or on Putin's specific travel plans in light of renewed strikes.
No Kremlin or Iranian government statement post-dating the July 8 ceasefire collapse addressing whether Caspian Summit plans are still on track; no confirmed Putin itinerary.

question: Will the FAO Food Price Index reach a new yearly high for July 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as **Yes** if Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations' [FAO Food Price Index](https://www.fao.org/worldfoodsituation/foodpricesindex/en/) value for July 2026, upon its initial release date, is higher than its level for any other month in 2026.
reference_class: FAO FFPI year-to-date-high hit rate by month, conditional on 2 prior down-months vs. presence of an active comparable supply shock
reasoning:
[pooled 3 independent runs [0.52, 0.27, 0.3] -> 0.357; the narrative below is the research run's own view (0.52)]
2026 YTD FFPI high is a plateau at ~130.7-130.8 (Apr/May); June already eased to 130.3 (-0.3%), a second straight monthly decline. July needs only ~0.4-0.5% more to set a new high - a low bar. But a fresh, comparable shock broke out July 7-10: Iran ceasefire collapsed, US struck Iran again, tankers attacked in Hormuz, shipping traffic 'ground to a halt' - the same mechanism that drove 3 straight FFPI increases in Feb-Apr. Wheat already up sharply in first half of July (though volatile, pulling back on strong China supply data). Meat continues climbing to record highs. Offsetting: palm oil fell in early July, sugar in a supply glut downtrend, dairy weak. Genuinely close to a coin flip - the catalyst is real and analogous to the earlier successful run-up, but pass-through often takes 1-2 months and started only days before question open, so August may capture more of the effect than July.
what_would_change_my_mind: Hormuz conflict de-escalates again quickly in mid/late July, letting energy and cereal quotations retreat as they did in May-June
Palm oil and dairy stabilize or rebound alongside cereals, removing the main drag
Wheat/cereal rally proves to be a brief spike that fully reverses before the monthly average is calculated
Direct sighting of Kalshi's kxfaofpi July contract price

missing_evidence: No FAO flash/nowcast estimate exists yet for July (release not due until 8/7); mid-to-late July component trajectory (days 12-31) is unknown

No FAO or private-sector nowcast for the July 2026 FFPI value itself exists yet (official release not due until 2026-08-07); the full-month price trajectory for the remaining ~20 days of July, and whether the war escalation persists or cools, is unresolved.

question: Will a Tucker Carlson-founded political party file FEC Form 1 before September 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as **Yes** if, after July 3, 2026 and before September 1, 2026, a Form 1 "Statement of Organization" is filed with the US [Federal Election Commission](https://www.fec.gov/data/filings/?data_type=processed) for any political party founded or co-founded by Tucker Carlson.
reference_class: Prominent political/media figures who publicly announce intent to found a new political party, converting to an actual FEC Form 1 filing naming them as founder within ~2 months
reasoning:
[pooled 4 independent runs [0.14, 0.12, 0.12, 0.08] -> 0.113; the narrative below is the research run's own view (0.14)]
Carlson (July 1-2, 2026) only voiced intent to 'help build' a third party, explicitly disclaiming a candidate role and giving no name, treasurer, structure, or timeline. As of July 11 (research date), 10 days later, no further concrete organizational steps have surfaced. Base rate: prominent-figure 'I'm starting a party' announcements that convert to an actual FEC Form 1 naming them as founder within ~8 weeks are uncommon unless the figure already has campaign-finance infrastructure ready to go (Yang/Forward Party filed same-day, but Yang had pre-built PAC/staff). Musk's 2025 'America Party' shows the filing itself is a trivial administrative step that CAN happen within days, but even there verified/legitimate registration was disputed and messy despite Musk publicly claiming he'd 'formed' the party. Carlson's non-candidate stance suggests he's positioning as a promoter/enabler rather than the hands-on organizer who files paperwork quickly. A separate 'We The People Party' exists but doesn't name Carlson as founder; MTG's parallel party talk is a distinct, not-yet-merged effort. Ballot-access deadlines for 2026 midterms have mostly passed, reducing urgency to file immediately (more likely aimed at 2028). Weighing the low technical bar and high media salience against the total absence of concrete steps and Carlson's own vagueness, I lean well below 50% but not negligible given 7 weeks remain and pressure could accelerate.
what_would_change_my_mind: A specific party name, treasurer, or committee announced by Carlson or his team
Reports of lawyers/staff being hired specifically for FEC registration
MTG or another organizationally-experienced figure formally merging efforts with Carlson under one named entity
Any FEC Form 1 filing appearing in FEC's processed filings database naming a party with Carlson credibly reported as founder

missing_evidence: No news after July 3, 2026 shows organizational progress (treasurer, lawyer, filing prep); could not confirm whether 'We The People Party' is formally tied to Carlson
No news after July 3, 2026 indicating any organizational progress (treasurer, lawyer, party name); no confirmation of merger with MTG or 'We The People Party' efforts
No news after July 3, 2026 on concrete organizing steps (treasurer, committee name, legal counsel) was found despite searching.

question: How many visitor arrivals will the government of Fiji report for July 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as the number of visitor arrivals reported by the Fiji Bureau of Statistics for July 2026, at its [Visitor Arrival Statistics](https://www.statsfiji.gov.fj/category/tourism-and-migration-statistics/visitor-arrivals-statistics/) portal.
reference_class: Fiji Bureau of Statistics monthly visitor arrivals, July month, prior-year actual adjusted by trailing 2026 YoY growth trend
reasoning: July is Fiji's peak season (record 99,311 in July 2025, +1.0% YoY). 2026 growth decelerated from a record Q1 (+7.0% YoY) to slightly negative in Apr (-0.8%) and May (-1.4%) 2026, per Fiji Bureau of Statistics releases, driven by elevated jet fuel prices, Middle East-linked supply risk, and Fiji Airways route suspensions (Fiji Times economist commentary, July 2026), with consensus outlook 'broadly flat' for full-year 2026 vs 2025's 986,367. Applying the recent -0.8%/-1.4% YoY trend to July 2025's 99,311 gives ~98,000-98,700; cross-checked via the June-to-July seasonal step-up (+7.1% in 2025) applied to an estimated flat-to-slightly-down June 2026 gives a similar ~98,400. No market (Polymarket/Kalshi) prices this question. Upside risk from continued strong AU (+17% YoY Q1) and NZ (+15% YoY Q1) demand, the two largest source markets (68% combined); downside risk from further fuel-driven capacity cuts.

question: Will Thomas Massie file to run for any elective office before August 25, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as **Yes** if, after July 9, 2026 and before August 25, 2026, [Thomas Massie](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thomas_Massie) files with any election authority to run for a public office in the 2026 election cycle. This may include, but is not limited to, any filing with the [FEC](https://www.fec.gov/help-candidates-and-committees/registering-candidate/house-senate-president-candidate-registration/) or any state filing such a declaration of intent to be a write-in candidate filed with the Commonwealth of Kentucky.
reference_class: Politicians subject to intense media speculation about filing for a different office within a ~7-week window absent a confirmed triggering event (opponent's seat vacancy)
reasoning:
[pooled 3 independent runs [0.17, 0.13, 0.13] -> 0.142; the narrative below is the research run's own view (0.17)]
Two paths to Yes: (1) McConnell vacancy before ~Aug 3 (needed for a 2026 special election) then Massie filing for it — P(vacancy in this narrow window)~15-20%, P(Massie files | vacancy)~35-45% given intense GOP-base pressure but no commitment, giving ~7%; (2) independent write-in filing for the Barr-Booker Senate race by the Aug 24 deadline — reported as of no interest yet, but Massie has precedent of filing low-cost 'optionality' paperwork (his May 2026 FEC 2028 filing), giving ~8-10%. Combined via rough independence ~15-18%. No firm current-cycle denial exists (his only explicit denial was stale, from May 2024, predating the primary loss and McConnell's health crisis).
what_would_change_my_mind: McConnell confirmed resignation or death before Aug 3, 2026
Any Massie statement explicitly ruling out a 2026 run
News of Massie filing FEC Senate committee paperwork or a KY write-in declaration
Reporting that Massie's team is actively organizing a campaign infrastructure

missing_evidence: No direct reporting on Massie's decision timeline or internal deliberations; no confirmed diagnosis/prognosis for McConnell; no polling on whether Massie would actually win a write-in or special-election bid, which could affect his willingness to file

question: Will Pete Ricketts lead Dan Osborn in the Nebraska Senate race polling on August 31, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as **Yes** if on August 31, 2026, Pete Ricketts leads Dan Osborn in the General Election Polling Average at the [2026 Senate Polling](https://www.racetothewh.com/senate/26) tracker at the RaceToTheWH website.
reference_class: Polling-average snapshots in competitive Senate races with a stable incumbent lead of several points over the prior 2+ months, ~7 weeks before the snapshot date
reasoning:
[pooled 4 independent runs [0.85, 0.85, 0.8, 0.85] -> 0.839; the narrative below is the research run's own view (0.85)]
PollsMax (comparable aggregator to the resolution source) showed Ricketts +4.7 (48.4/43.7) as of July 12, 2026, stable since May with no new polls in two months. Individual ballot-tests are tighter (48/47, sometimes tied, one D-aligned poll reportedly showing Osborn ahead), but the composite average has consistently favored Ricketts. Nebraska's strong GOP lean, Ricketts' incumbency/self-funding, and prediction markets pricing Republicans ~61-62% to win the seat outright (vs Independent ~30-35%) all support Ricketts as favorite to also lead the Aug 31 polling-average snapshot. Main risk: thin poll sample (5 polls) makes the average sensitive to one new Osborn-favorable poll before the measurement date, and several individual polls already show a true statistical tie.
what_would_change_my_mind: A new poll (especially from a neutral/Osborn-aligned house) showing Osborn ahead by several points, added to the average before Aug 31
A swing in fundamentals (major Ricketts scandal, Osborn fundraising surge, national environment shift) between now and Aug 31
Direct confirmation that racetothewh's own average currently differs materially from PollsMax's

missing_evidence: Could not directly retrieve live racetothewh.com Nebraska polling-average figures (JS-gated site); relying on comparable PollsMax aggregator as proxy.
Could not directly retrieve live racetothewh.com polling average figures (JS-rendered site); relying on comparable aggregator (PollsMax) and individual poll reports as proxies.
No live read of the actual resolution source (racetothewh.com/senate/26) was obtainable this run; relied on PollsMax as a proxy aggregator.

question: What will be the total tokenized real-world asset (RWA) value on Solana on July 23, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves to the total tokenized real-world asset (RWA) value on the Solana blockchain as reported by the RWA.xyz Solana network dashboard (https://app.rwa.xyz/networks/solana), measured as the sum of the "Distributed Asset Value" and "Represented Asset Value" headline figures shown for Solana, using the values displayed for the "as-of" date of July 23, 2026 (or, if that exact date is not selectable, the latest available snapshot dated on or before July 23, 2026). The answer is expressed in billions of US dollars: for example, a Distributed Asset Value of $3.29B plus a Represented Asset Value of $125.86M sums to $3.42B and resolves as 3.42.
reference_class: Month-over-month growth rate of tokenized RWA value on Solana (RWA.xyz Distributed+Represented) during its 2026 H1 growth phase
reasoning: Current RWA.xyz Solana total (Distributed $3.28B + Represented $0.126B) is ~$3.41B as of July 13, nearly flat vs $3.42B on July 11 after a rapid spike from ~$2.8B (May) to ~$3.4B (July 1). Longer-run monthly growth was ~10-20%, but has visibly decelerated in the most recent 48 hours. Base rate from the May-July trend (~10%/month) applied to the 10-day window to July 23 implies roughly +3-4% growth absent new catalysts, with upside risk from active issuer ramp-up (Spiko/Amundi just launched July 2, BUIDL, Ondo tokenized equities) and limited downside since RWA inflows rarely reverse sharply short of a specific redemption event.

question: Will SK Hynix release its Q2 2026 earnings before July 25, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves YES if SK Hynix Inc. (KRX: 000660) publicly releases its second-quarter fiscal-year-2026 earnings results (covering the three months ending June 30, 2026) before July 25, 2026, Korea Standard Time. The release is confirmed by the appearance of a "SK hynix FY2026 Q2 Earnings Results" entry (or the equivalent quarterly operating-profit figure) on the company's Investor Relations "Earnings Release" page (https://www.skhynix.com/ir/UI-FR-IR01/), by a corresponding regulatory filing on Korea's DART system (https://dart.fss.or.kr), or by reporting of the released Q2 2026 figures from [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions). It resolves NO if no such Q2 2026 earnings release has occurred before July 25, 2026 KST.
reference_class: SK Hynix quarterly (Q2) earnings release timing relative to a July 25 cutoff, historical 2020-2025
reasoning:
[pooled 4 independent runs [0.18, 0.1, 0.12, 0.14] -> 0.132; the narrative below is the research run's own view (0.18)]
No official SK Hynix confirmation of Q2 2026 earnings date as of Jul 13, 2026. Historical trend (Jul 25 in 2024, Jul 24 in 2025, each ~1 day earlier YoY; Q1 2026 gap of 23 days from quarter-end) would extrapolate to ~Jul 23, 2026, favoring YES. However, multiple independent-looking but likely commonly-sourced (FnGuide-style Korean corporate calendar) Korean financial press articles from early July 2026 (news1.kr, jbnews, newsis, fnnews) specifically and repeatedly cite ~Jul 28-29 as the expected release date, plausibly delayed by SK Hynix's major Nasdaq ADR listing (priced Jul 9, traded from Jul 10, raised $26.5B) which likely consumed significant IR/finance/legal bandwidth in the preceding weeks. Western aggregator sites (Investing.com, Finbox, TipRanks) showing Jul 22 appear to be generic algorithmic placeholders rather than Korea-specific sourced estimates. Weighing the more specific, recent, and mechanistically-plausible Jul 28-29 estimates over the historical extrapolation and algorithmic guesses.
what_would_change_my_mind: An official SK Hynix IR calendar posting or DART filing confirming a specific conference call date
A Yonhap/Reuters/Bloomberg wire report with a firm, company-sourced date
SK Hynix's historical practice of filing a conference-call notice ~1 week ahead — if that notice appears before Jul 20 naming a date ≤Jul 24, that would flip this toward YES

missing_evidence: No official SK Hynix IR calendar posting or DART filing naming a confirmed Q2 2026 date; the July 29 date remains an analyst/press expectation, not company-confirmed

No official SK Hynix IR/DART confirmation of the exact Q2 2026 date; unclear whether the widely-cited 'Jul 29' figure traces to a single shared financial-data vendor or independent analyst/company channel checks.
No official company-confirmed date found from any source; all July 29 citations trace to press/analyst 'expectation' framing rather than a company announcement

question: Will Solana (SOL) close above $85 on any day between July 11 and July 23, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves YES if the daily closing price of Solana (SOL) in US dollars, as reported on the [CoinGecko Solana historical data page](https://www.coingecko.com/en/coins/solana/historical_data), is strictly above $85.00 for at least one calendar day (UTC) from July 11, 2026 through July 23, 2026, inclusive. It resolves NO if every daily "Close" value in that window is $85.00 or lower. If CoinGecko is unavailable, the daily close reported by another major price tracker (e.g. CoinMarketCap) or [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) will be used.
reference_class: SOL closing above a resistance level ~9-11% above spot within an ~11-trading-day window, during a range-bound, bearish-tilted period with elevated on-chain activity
reasoning:
[pooled 3 independent runs [0.16, 0.14, 0.13] -> 0.143; the narrative below is the research run's own view (0.16)]
SOL spot ~$77.4 (July 12-13), essentially flat vs July 10's $78.07 — July 11/12 closes almost certainly already below $85, leaving ~11 days remaining needing a ~9-11% rally. Calibrating implied daily vol (~2.8%) from Polymarket's monthly touch-$90 market (33% over 31 days) and applying a reflection-principle random-walk estimate for max-of-daily-closes over 11 days gives ~28-31% for a 'touch-equivalent' probability of clearing $85. Adjusting down for (a) the close-not-touch requirement, (b) a well-documented $79-85 'supply wall' resistance zone that multiple independent analysts flag as a likely rejection point, and (c) a soft macro backdrop (BTC down ~20% in June, bearish technicals 24:5), while adjusting up slightly for genuinely bullish on-chain metrics (active addresses near yearly highs), yields a final estimate around 16%.
what_would_change_my_mind: A daily close above $80 in the next few days, which technicians say would open the path toward $100+
A broad crypto risk-on rally (BTC reclaiming $68k+) that lifts SOL with it
A SOL-specific catalyst (ETF news, major protocol upgrade, large inflow data) breaking the $79-85 supply wall
Confirmation that July 11 or 12 already closed above $85 (would flip this toward near-certain YES)

missing_evidence: No market found with a contract matching this exact $85/close/Jul11-23 window; relied on Polymarket's differently-shaped 'touch $90' market for implied-vol calibration only.

No window-matched (Jul11-23 specifically) prediction market was found on Polymarket/Kalshi/Manifold; relied on adjacent full-month touch-based markets for implied volatility, which imperfectly match this question's close-based, shorter-window mechanic.

question: Will Uganda report a 21st confirmed Ebola case between July 11 and July 24, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves YES if a World Health Organization Disease Outbreak News (DON) update, or a report from [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions), reports that Uganda's cumulative number of confirmed Ebola (Bundibugyo virus) cases in the 2026 outbreak has risen to 21 or more at any point between July 11, 2026 and July 24, 2026 (inclusive). It resolves NO if the cumulative confirmed case count for Uganda remains at 20 through July 24, 2026. WHO Disease Outbreak News is published at https://www.who.int/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news (look for the item titled "Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo & Uganda"). Only laboratory-confirmed cases attributed to Uganda count; probable cases, suspected cases, and cases attributed to the Democratic Republic of the Congo do not.
reference_class: weekly probability of a new Uganda Ebola case emergence during the 'monitoring/no active contacts' phase of a cross-border-linked EVD outbreak, conditioned on 3 consecutive case-free weeks
reasoning:
[pooled 3 independent runs [0.13, 0.11, 0.13] -> 0.123; the narrative below is the research run's own view (0.13)]
Uganda has held at 20 confirmed cases since the last one on 21 June 2026 (3 weeks flat as of 12 July), with contact-tracing complete (821/831 cleared) and surveillance now passive/alert-based. This favors NO. However, the source epidemic in DRC (Ituri/North Kivu) is not winding down — 1,792 cases with 625 deaths as of 9 July, +33 cases in the latest period — and 15 of Uganda's 20 cases were DRC-linked imports via the same corridor, so the importation pathway remains live. Using a Beta(0.5,0.5) prior updated on 3 case-free weeks gives ~12-15% weekly emergence risk persisting into a 2-week window; blending down from the naive higher figure given documented surveillance improvements yields ~13%.
what_would_change_my_mind: Any WHO DON or Uganda MoH daily update reporting a new lab-confirmed case in Kampala/Wakiso
A retroactive report of an onset/report date between 11-24 July 2026
News of a new DRC-Uganda border crossing incident or symptomatic traveler under investigation
WHO announcing the 42-day countdown clock (running toward ~2 Aug) without interruption, strengthening the case for NO

missing_evidence: No direct confirmation of current border-screening protocol effectiveness or traveler volume data from Ituri/North Kivu into Kampala for the July 11-24 window

question: What will TAC chain's DeFi TVL (per DefiLlama) be on July 23, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as the "Total Value Locked" (TVL) figure displayed for the TAC chain on its DefiLlama dashboard at https://defillama.com/chain/TAC, as attributed to July 23, 2026 (UTC). Enter the value in whole US dollars — for example, a displayed TVL of $0.84m resolves as 840000. If DefiLlama displays the value abbreviated (e.g., "$0.84m"), use the underlying dollar figure shown for the July 23, 2026 data point on the chain's TVL chart. If DefiLlama is temporarily unavailable on that date, the daily TVL value it reports for July 23, 2026 once service resumes will be used. If DefiLlama permanently ceases to publish a TVL figure for the TAC chain, the question is annulled.
reference_class: Small-cap L1/L2 chains post security-exploit + token-price collapse, TVL trajectory ~10-14 days after the shock before finding a stable floor
reasoning: DefiLlama's historical API shows TAC TVL falling in discrete step-downs tied to shocks (May bridge exploit; July 8 Binance Alpha listing crash that sent TAC token down 90% intraday), from ~$1.78M (Jul 1) to $816K (Jul 12), interspersed with brief plateaus of ±1-3%/day. Token remains extremely thin and volatile ($0.0038-0.0046), so further shock-driven step-downs are plausible before Jul 23, but most speculative/hot capital has likely already exited given the 99.6% decline from the Aug-2025 peak. Central case is continued moderate bleed (~15-20% further decline) with fat tails on both sides given the demonstrated volatility. No human prediction market exists on this niche question.

question: Will Susan Collins lead the Democratic candidate in the Maine Senate race polling on August 31, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as **Yes** if on August 31, 2026, Susan Collins leads the Democratic nominee in the General Election Polling Average at the [2026 Senate Polling](https://www.racetothewh.com/senate/26) tracker at the RaceToTheWH website.
reference_class: Polling-average leads for endangered incumbent senators facing a freshly-nominated challenger, ~5 weeks post-nomination
reasoning:
[pooled 4 independent runs [0.32, 0.28, 0.33, 0.4] -> 0.331; the narrative below is the research run's own view (0.32)]
Platner withdrew July 10 after rape allegations; new Dem nominee chosen via convention July 25-27. Current hypothetical matchup polls: Jackson (nomination favorite, ~70% on Polymarket) leads Collins 49-44; Bellows tied 47-47; Shah D+1. Win markets price Democrat ~63-64% to win seat. Weighting by nomination odds gives ~30% Collins leads avg; adjusted up slightly for Collins' history of outperforming polls (2020 comp) and for aggregation-transition noise since the site's average will be re-basing to a brand-new nominee mid-window.

A wave of Collins-favorable house-effect polls (e.g., Republican-aligned pollsters) enters the average
Evidence Collins is running the same crossover/split-ticket strength she showed in 2020 despite the topline environment

question: Will NVIDIA (NVDA) close above $225 on any trading day from July 20 to July 24, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves YES if the official Nasdaq closing price of NVIDIA Corporation (ticker NVDA) common stock exceeds $225.00 on at least one U.S. equity trading day from July 20, 2026 through July 24, 2026, inclusive. It resolves NO otherwise. Resolution uses the daily "Close" value shown in the historical price table at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/NVDA/history/ , or [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) if Yahoo Finance is unavailable. For example, a close of $226.50 on any of those days resolves YES; if the highest close across all five days is $220.00, it resolves NO.
reference_class: NVDA 5-trading-day forward windows requiring a ~6-7% close-to-close gain, given ~2.2%/day realized volatility
reasoning: NVDA closed $210.96 on Jul 10, 2026 (+4% that day, +7.8% over 2wks), needing +6.65% to exceed $225 during Jul20-24 (days 5-9 ahead). Daily realized vol ~2.18% (14-session sample) implies via GBM numerical integration ~29% chance the running max close over that 5-day window exceeds $225; nudged to ~31% for positive momentum/bullish analyst consensus ($285-305 targets), offset by mean-reversion risk post-pop and no in-window earnings catalyst. Stale Polymarket month-ahead odds (from a ~$195-200 base) back-validate the vol assumption but aren't a clean current anchor for this specific window/threshold.
what_would_change_my_mind: A favorable U.S.-China H200 export ruling landing before Jul 20 (bullish shock)
A negative H200/China headline or broad AI-chip selloff before the window (would push required move further out of reach)
Confirmation of a live, current (post-Jul 10) prediction-market price near this threshold
Any pre-window close already near/above $220, which would sharply raise the odds
